steps.title: Identify the key temporal signal
steps.content: The passage provides two specific dates: February 14, 1927 (when Vernonia liatroides was collected) and May 3, 1930 (when Achyrocline vauthieriana was collected). These dates reveal a clear temporal relationship between the two collections.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D uses “after collecting Vernonia liatroides in February of 1927” to show that the 1930 sample came later — the word “after” directly expresses the temporal sequence.

The other three choices focus on location (A), provide a single date in isolation (B), or emphasize geography and plant family rather than timing (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
